_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of the structure, architecture, and design of modern wireless communications systems.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP, DNS resolution, HTTP/HTTPS protocols, firewall and security group configurations, load balancing, and how network architecture affects data pipeline design and performance.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-013 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-K-008 (OP, cosine: 0.328), SP-K-010 (SP, cosine: 0.323).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.3/10 (raw 0.526). Shared vocabulary: [architecture, design, knowledge, systems]. DCWF-only terms [communications, modern, structure, wireless] vs WIDAI-only terms [affects, balancing, configurations, data, dns, firewall] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.891 (P=0.924, R=0.861). P exceeds R by 0.062 — the DCWF element's 12 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 31 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (97.5%). "Knowledge of the structure, architecture, and design of modern wireless communic" and "Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP," are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.388 < STS 0.526 (gap -0.138). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [communications, modern, structure] and [affects, balancing, configurations] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.1%. Shared tokens: [architecture, design, knowledge, systems]. DCWF-only: [communications, modern, structure, wireless]. WIDAI-only: [affects, balancing, configurations, data, dns, firewall].
